name: agents-dont-read-errors-trajectories
description: Trial trajectories from a counterfactual evaluation of error-message reading in tool-using LLM agents. 2,600 trials across four open-weight models (Mistral-7B, Llama-3.1-8B, Qwen2.5-14B, Qwen3-14B) on the MBPP-sanitized benchmark, under four error-content conditions (Real, Scrambled, Misleading, Empty). Each record contains the full agent trajectory (turns, tool calls, errors, recoveries), per-trial metrics (ERR, ECR, success), and the error-substitution metadata for that condition.
citeAs: Anonymous Authors. Agents Don't Read Errors: Counterfactual Scrambling for Testing Error-Message Use in Tool-Using LLMs. NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations and Datasets Track (under review).
rai:dataCollection: Trajectories were collected by running each of four open-weight LLMs (served via Ollama) on 100 MBPP-sanitized programming tasks under four error-content conditions. The tool environment intercepts tool errors and substitutes the message body according to the assigned condition before returning to the agent. All inference was deterministic (temperature=0).
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: No human annotation. Per-trial task success was scored by an automated unit-test runner using the canonical MBPP test cases.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: Released as a single tagged release on the anonymized GitHub repository. We do not plan ongoing updates; the dataset corresponds to a fixed experimental run reported in the paper.
rai:dataLimitations: Limited to MBPP-sanitized (Python coding tasks); four open-weight models up to 14B parameters; no proprietary frontier models. Qwen3-14B engaged with the tool environment on only ~42% of trials, so per-cell sample sizes are smaller for that model. Scrambling preserves token length and structural cues but cannot guarantee that all latent semantic signal is removed.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Findings inform deployment of reasoning-tuned LLMs as agents but do not present novel capabilities or deployment risks. The dataset contains only model outputs on public coding problems and presents no privacy concerns.
rai:dataBiases: The MBPP-sanitized benchmark consists of short Python programming problems and may not reflect agent behavior on long-context or domain-specific code. The four models are open-weight only and do not represent the full population of deployed LLM agents.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. Trajectories contain only model outputs on public benchmark problems.

distribution.name: 20260428T081951Z-06f3bf77_results.jsonl
distribution.description: Original sweep: Mistral-7B, Qwen2.5-14B, Qwen3-14B (1024-token budget) on MBPP and GAIA, 1,800 trials.

distribution.name: 20260428T174000Z-6e3d244c_results.jsonl
distribution.description: Qwen3-14B replication run at 8,192-token budget, 400 trials.

recordSet.name: trials
recordSet.description: One record per agent trial. Each record is a complete agent trajectory plus per-trial metrics and the experimental context (model, condition, task).

field.name: status
field.description: Trial status. 'ok' means the trial completed (with or without solving the task); 'failed' means the platform errored out.

field.name: context.model
field.description: Model identifier: mistral_7b, llama3_1_8b, qwen2_5_14b, qwen3_14b.

field.name: context.condition
field.description: Counterfactual error-content condition: real, scrambled_plausible, misleading, empty.

field.name: context.dataset
field.description: Source benchmark: mbpp or gaia_validation. Primary analysis uses mbpp only.

field.name: metrics.task_success
field.description: 1.0 if the agent's final answer passed all unit tests for the task; 0.0 otherwise.

field.name: metrics.err
field.description: Error Recovery Rate for this trial: n_recoveries / n_errors. Null when n_errors = 0.

field.name: metrics.n_errors
field.description: Count of tool-error turns in the trajectory.

field.name: metrics.n_recoveries
field.description: Count of error-turns immediately followed by a successful tool call.

field.name: trajectory.turns
field.description: List of turns in the agent trajectory. Each turn has a kind (system, user, assistant, tool_call, tool_result, tool_error), tool_name, tool_args, content, and metadata. For tool_error turns, the substituted (visible-to-agent) content is stored in 'content' and the original error is stored in 'original_error'.
